GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "These are the must-have trends for summer", "Think clever cut-outs and sorbet-soft hues to lounge in in the sun.", "Set against the scenic landscape of the Southern Highlands, David Jones unveiled its first-ever virtual runway show at the Seidler House on October 30. The innovative and shoppable presentation, which included Georgia Fowler and Cameron Stephens in the model line-up, showcased the key trends to try and buy ahead of summer, as styled by Vogue's fashion editor and market director, Philippa Moroney. A slew of international and local designers–from Scandi-cool It-girl label Ganni to French favourite Isabel Marant and Australian go-tos Zimmermann, Bassike and Camilla–set the stage for a summer predominated by warm, earthy tones, playful puff sleeves and pattern and print. These worked seamlessly with the show's natural backdrop, reflecting a unique synergy noted by Moroney. She says: "I love the idea of tonal dressing inspired by the Australian landscape with a nod to utility with strong anchored accessories in footwear, and eyewear in complementary shades." This sense of practicality was factored in a carob-coloured boiler suit from Acler and ecru cargo-style shorts by Isabel Marant Etoile, that can be pared back with everyday essentials. But a spirit of embracing the warmth of summer, as temperatures heat up, was palpable too. Moroney identified a key trend she dubbed 'colour crush' on the runway. "Pop colour and resort dressing go hand-in-hand," she says. "This season, sunset colours of hot pink, orange, reds and deep honey are key for updating your look. Choose flowing printed dresses or a bold swim silhouette." These pigmented hues were evidenced in a coral Bianca Spender dress and elsewhere, a subtle nod to the trend came via a soft pink wrap dress by Copenhagen-based cult label Rotate, that also reflected the ubiquity of billowing and puff sleeves this season. Moroney identifies this trend as a personal favourite. "This time [balloon or puff sleeves are rendered] in a pastel colour palette to have you up in the clouds with dream silhouettes by Aje and Camilla and Marc," she says. What about a standout look of the season? "Georgia Fowler in the bassike two-piece. This batik print is modernised in this directional top which showcases a great pair of shoulders and is perfectly balanced by the balloon pants with ankle ties that are cool and comfortable." Try them on for size by shopping from David Jones's exclusive range of both Australian and international designers, available for purchase straight from the virtual runway online or in-store."
